titleStmt.title: SII 11.19: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 19. (B.K. No. 59 of 1926-27). SIRUṀJA, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON A HERO-STONE SET UP NEAR THE SCHOOL.

summary.p: This damaged inscription belongs to the reign of Akālavarsha (Kṛishṇa II) and is dated in Śaka 805 (= A.D. 883). It records the death of a certain Eṟeyamman in a cattle raid at [Ni]vudi.
This is the earliest inscription known so far of the reign of Kṛishṇa II for whom A.D. 888 was the earliest date found hitherto (see Kielhorn's Southern List, No. 81).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.note: The records have since been published by Mr. N.Lakshminarayan Rao,Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XXI, pp. 206 ff.
div.p:
svastyakāla varṣaśrī pṛthuvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēśvara bhaṭara
rssakalarājyam Abhivṛddhige saluttumire sakavarṣa Eṇṭunūṟa
Aydaneya varṣaṁ pravarttisuttire kiḍalegadhipāṇnan Eṟeyammaṁ nivuditoṟugoḷoḷkā
di sattōn Ī kalla śrī butēṁdragāvuṇḍaṁ kommaguṟeyammaṁ niṟisidōr .
Digital edition of SII 11.19 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
